title: 4 surprising things to know about abortion in America since Dobbs
description: A confusing patchwork of state laws began to take shape hours after the Supreme Court ruled to overturn Roe v. Wade on June 24, 2022. Here's where things stand now on the abortion issue.

title: Trump keeps sabotaging legislation over a voting bill. Here's what's in it
description: President Trump blew up what could have been a win for his party — and he did it to force lawmakers to pass an elections overhaul bill that has been all but doomed in the Senate.

title: White House seeks $88 billion from Congress for Iran war
description: The request mostly includes money for the Pentagon, with $67 billion going toward replenishing the military’s stocks of munitions and the cost of sending so many forces to the Middle East.

title: Trump abruptly cancels signing of bipartisan bill on affordable housing
description: The president posted on social media that the signing was off until the Senate passes his election integrity bill. It is unclear whether he intends to kill the housing bill.

title: Socialist election wins spawn wider identity questions for Democrats
description: Tuesday’s primary victories for a slate of leftist candidates endorsed by New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani foretell bigger battles for a Democratic Party trying to win back power nationally.

title: Democratic socialist won N.Y. primary despite scrutiny over inflammatory posts
description: Darializa Avila Chevalier, a first-time candidate who unseated a House Democrat, has spent years advocating for Palestinians.

title: Hegseth thwarted internal efforts to extend key Army general’s career
description: Gen. Christopher Donahue, seen as a top warfighter, is the latest apparent casualty in a purge of senior military leaders by the Trump administration.

title: Trump cancels signing ceremony for bipartisan housing bill, again upending GOP agenda
description: Republicans hoped to celebrate passage of a bipartisan spending bill on Wednesday — until President Trump scuttled their plans. It was the latest in a series of rifts between Trump and his party.

title: Mayor Mamdani endorsed three House candidates. They all won
description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Zohran Mamdani, the mayor of New York City, the day after all three Congressional candidates he endorsed won their Democratic primary elections.

title: Trump upends bipartisan housing bill, leaving lawmakers scrambling
description: President Trump has cancelled a signing planned for today of the 21st Century Road to Housing Act, the largest housing affordability in decades passed with overwhelming bipartisan support in Congress.

title: Democratic socialists are winning key races in cities. What that label means.
description: Zohran Mamdani won the New York mayor contest last year, and two candidates he and the Democratic Socialists of America backed have now won U.S. House primaries.

title: Postal Service says its cash crisis is delayed until at least 2031, but problems loom
description: The U.S. Postal Service is no longer set to be out of cash in 2027, the agency's head says. But its finances remain shaky as Trump officials keep putting it in political hot water.

title: Will Texas' new top voting official be a 'disruptor'? Locals are preparing for it
description: Just ahead of closely contested midterms, Texas is about to get a new top voting official. Many locals there fear the frontrunner is a state lawmaker and pastor with no election experience.

title: The 4 GOP senators who broke ranks and voted to block Trump from resuming Iran war
description: The president seethed at Susan Collins, Bill Cassidy, Lisa Murkowski and Rand Paul after the war powers resolution passed, saying: “I will get it done, one way or the other.”

title: Understanding 'masculinism,' a movement to restore the primacy of men
description: Masculinism is a belief that feminism emasculates men, and men should be in control while women stay at home raising children. The Atlantic writer Helen Lewis says the movement is becoming mainstream.

title: Mamdani emerges as kingmaker, and other takeaways from Tuesday’s primaries
description: Democratic socialist candidates won key races in New York, while more establishment candidates prevailed in other contests.

title: Congress passes major housing bill. And, Mamdani-backed candidates sweep NYC primaries
description: Congress has passed the largest housing affordability bill in decades. And all three candidates endorsed by New York City mayor Zohran Mamdani have won their primaries.

title: Trump to meet with Senate Republican as frustrations rise within the party
description: Divides over what GOP priorities should be ahead of this fall's midterm elections are testing the relationship between President Trump and Senate Majority Leader John Thune.

title: Mamdani-backed candidates sweep House primaries in NYC. See live results.
description: Three left-wing hopefuls backed by New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani won their races, including two who unseated incumbents.

title: Supreme Court says Rastafarian can’t sue prison officials over shorn dreadlocks
description: The ruling dealt with legal technicalities but is a departure from a series of decisions by the justices expanding religious freedoms in recent terms.

title: Keir Starmer is out. Other establishment politicians are in trouble.
description: Across the West’s advanced democracies, conventional political parties are being gutted by stagnant wages and fraying social contracts that have left voters fed up with the status quo.
